messages.content:
The qualifications for becoming president are set forth by

Group of answer choices

each political party.

the U.S. Constitution.

Congress.

members of the cabinet.

The qualifications for becoming president are set forth by the U.S. Constitution. 

According to Article II, Section 1, Clause 5 of the Constitution, a person must meet the following criteria to be eligible for the presidency:

1. Must be a natural-born citizen of the United States.
2. Must be at least 35 years old.
3. Must have been a resident within the United States for 14 years.
Only type in the correct answer
the U.S. Constitution.

To be elected president of the United States, a person must

Group of answer choices

have been a U.S. resident for 20 years.

be at least 45 years old.

be college educated.

have been a U.S. resident for 14 years.
have been a U.S. resident for 14 years.

The only president to be elected to four terms in office is

Group of answer choices

George Washington.

Theodore Roosevelt.

Franklin D. Roosevelt.

James Madison.
Franklin D. Roosevelt.

Which of the following is not a responsibility of the vice president?

Group of answer choices

The vice president must avoid taking a role in creating domestic policies.

The vice president is often sent overseas by the president to represent the United States.

The vice president's job as defined in the Constitution is to preside over the Senate.

The vice president must meet the same constitutional qualifications as the president.
The vice president must avoid taking a role in creating domestic policies.

Who said "A house divided against itself cannot stand."?

Group of answer choices

Abraham Lincoln

Franklin D. Roosevelt

John F. Kennedy

George W. Bush
Abraham Lincoln

If the vice president succeeds to the presidency, the

Group of answer choices

Speaker of the House becomes the new vice president.

new president chooses a new vice president, who must then be approved by Congress.

president pro tempore becomes the new vice president.

position of vice president remains empty until the next election.
new president chooses a new vice president, who must then be approved by Congress.

If the president pro tempore became president, which of the following would be correct?

Group of answer choices

The president and vice president had died, resigned, or been removed from office, and the Speaker of the House was out of the country.

The Speaker of the House did not belong to the same political party as the president.

The president pro tempore had been in office longer than the Speaker of the House.

The president, vice president, and Speaker of the House had died, resigned, or been removed from office.
The president, vice president, and Speaker of the House had died, resigned, or been removed from office.

In the official role of chief executive, the president is responsible for

Group of answer choices

writing new laws.

approving treaties with other nations.

running the space program.

appointing key federal officials.
appointing key federal officials.

The president suggests new programs and policies in an annual

Group of answer choices

Congressional Address.

State of the Union Address.

Judicial Address.

address to the Joint Chiefs of Staff.
State of the Union Address.

When visiting the leaders of other countries, the president is carrying out the role of chief

Group of answer choices

diplomat.

executive.

of state.

of staff.
diplomat.

As commander in chief of the armed forces, the president

Group of answer choices

leads forces into battle.

can send U.S. troops abroad.

can suspend the War Powers Act.

can declare war on another nation.
can send U.S. troops abroad.

Under the Constitution

Group of answer choices

only Congress can declare war.

only the president can declare war.

the president and Congress must declare war together.

the president's cabinet must approve all war declarations.
only Congress can declare war.

When the president appoints federal judges, approval must be given by

Group of answer choices

a majority vote from both the House and the Senate.

a majority vote from the House.

a majority vote from the Senate.

majority votes from both the House and the Senate and approval from the vice president.
a majority vote from the Senate.

A presidential pardon requires the approval of

Group of answer choices

a majority vote from both the House and the Senate.

a majority vote from the House.

a majority vote from the Senate.

none of the above
none of the above

Which of the following is NOT a power of the president?

Group of answer choices

proposing laws and programs to Congress

raising taxes

preparing the federal government's budget

appointing cabinet members, ambassadors, and federal judges
raising taxes

The Executive Office of the President includes

Group of answer choices

the Supreme Court.

the attorney general.

the president's closest political advisors.

the members of Congress.
the president's closest political advisors.

Executive departments

Group of answer choices

are strictly outlined in the Constitution.

are permanent departments established by the framers.

can be established, reorganized, or eliminated by Congress.

have become increasingly insignificant since the early 1900s.
can be established, reorganized, or eliminated by Congress.

Cabinet meetings are led by

Group of answer choices

the president.

the Speaker of the House.

the secretary of state.

the majority whip.
the president.

In the executive branch today, there are

Group of answer choices

5 departments.

15 departments.

only 25 people who assist the president.

only 75 people who assist the president.
15 departments.

In the executive branch today, there are

Group of answer choices

5 departments.

15 departments.

only 25 people who assist the president.

only 75 people who assist the president.
15 departments.

Which of the following is part of the Executive Office of the President?

Group of answer choices

Congress

Council of the Ten

FBI

National Security Council
National Security Council

Foreign policy is the special responsibility of

Group of answer choices

the Department of the Interior.

the National Security Council.

the Department of Defense.

the Department of State.
the Department of State.

The army, navy, and air force are part of

Group of answer choices

the Department of War.

the Department of Justice.

the Department of Defense.

the Department of Veterans Affairs.
the Department of Defense.

The Department of State is responsible for all of the following EXCEPT

Group of answer choices

issuing passports.

conducting foreign relations.

protecting U.S. citizens abroad.

enforcing immigration laws.
enforcing immigration laws.

All of the following are departments of the executive branch EXCEPT the Department of

Group of answer choices

Energy.

Aeronautics.

Education.

Veterans Affairs.
Aeronautics.

A citizen is concerned about helping the homeless of her community find decent places to live. Her local city council asks her to seek federal funds to support her ideas and programs. Which executive department should she contact?

Group of answer choices

the Department of Housing and Urban Development

the Department of Civil Rights

the Department of Treasury

the Department of Justice
the Department of Housing and Urban Development

The executive department responsible for protecting the nation against terrorist attacks and providing federal assistance when natural disasters occur is the Department of

Group of answer choices

Justice.

Defense.

Homeland Security.

Health and Human Services.
Homeland Security.

The U.S. Commission on Civil Rights is an example of a(n)

Group of answer choices

independent agency.

regulatory commission.

executive department.

none of the above
independent agency.

A candidate believes that an opponent violated federal election laws in a recent election. If he wants a federal investigation to be conducted, he should contact the

Group of answer choices

Department of Energy.

Interstate Environment Commission.

Commission on Civil Rights.

Federal Election Commission.
Federal Election Commission.

Regulatory commissions

Group of answer choices

are independent of the executive departments.

make rules but cannot enforce them.

have exactly the same function as independent agencies.

are headed by chairpersons elected by the people.
are independent of the executive departments.

Which term describes all of the employees of the federal agencies and executive departments?

Group of answer choices

diplomats

commission

bureaucracy

ambassadors
bureaucracy

The writers of the Constitution established a bicameral legislature in order to

Group of answer choices

provide equal representation in one house for all of the states.

streamline the process whereby a bill becomes a law.

prevent Congress from imitating the British Parliament.

make it more difficult for Congress to declare war in the future.
provide equal representation in one house for all of the states.

What is true about a direct democracy on a national level?

Group of answer choices

It is not a practical way to run the government.

It is necessary to elect others to represent us.

It is not possible for citizens to vote on every issue.

all of the above
all of the above

Congress determines how the seats in the House are to be apportioned

Group of answer choices

every ten years, after the census is taken.

every two years, after the elections are held.

based on voter turnout after each presidential election.

based on how many members from each political party are already in office.
every ten years, after the census is taken.

Regular elections for members of the House of Representatives are held

Group of answer choices

in January of each even-numbered year.

in November of each even-numbered year.

in April every four years.

in November of each odd-numbered year.
in November of each even-numbered year.

If a state's population decreases, its number of senators

Group of answer choices

decreases proportionately.

stays the same.

increases by one.

is sent to the Supreme Court for evaluation.
stays the same.

To ensure that the majority of all senators are experienced,

Group of answer choices

senators who have served more than four years are required to chair all committees.

all senators are guaranteed two terms in office.

only one-third of the senators are up for reelection every two years.

new senators are elected for eight-year terms.
only one-third of the senators are up for reelection every two years.

Which of the following is not a characteristic of the Senate?

Group of answer choices

Senators serve six-year terms.

The Senate is limited to 435 members.

Elections are held every two years.

There are two senators from each state.
The Senate is limited to 435 members.

Both houses of Congress have specific guidelines regarding

Group of answer choices

age of members.

citizenship of members.

legal residence of members.

all of the above
all of the above

A 32-year-old member of Congress has recently been reelected for her second term of office. It may be assumed that this person is

Group of answer choices

a senator.

a representative.

either a senator or a representative.

neither a senator nor a representative.
a representative.

Members of the House of Representatives must be all of the following EXCEPT:

Group of answer choices

at least 25 years old.

a U.S. citizen for at least seven years.

a legal resident of the state they represent.

have first served a term as a senator.
have first served a term as a senator.

Which of the following statements is false?

Group of answer choices

Members of Congress are limited to two terms.

Congress has a code of conduct for its members to follow.

Members of Congress are paid a fixed yearly salary.

Congress has immunity from its members being arrested while it is in session.
Members of Congress are limited to two terms.

Censured members of Congress

Group of answer choices

must petition the president for re-entry into the House or the Senate.

are no longer allowed to vote on proposed bills.

must stand alone at the front of the House or Senate as charges are read against them.

must serve brief jail time and pay a fine.
must stand alone at the front of the House or Senate as charges are read against them.

The function of each party's caucus is to

Group of answer choices

research proposed bills for select committees.

choose party leaders.

set priorities for committee business.

assign bills to committees.
choose party leaders.

If the president pro tempore is presiding in the Senate, it may be assumed that the

Group of answer choices

president is absent.

majority leader is absent.

vice president is absent.

floor leader is absent.
vice president is absent.

Whose job is it in Congress to count votes, encourage party loyalty, and ensure that the party’s members are present for important votes?

Group of answer choices

president pro tempore

Speaker of the House

majority leader

party whip
party whip

The Speaker of the House

Group of answer choices

is the most powerful officer in Congress.

is appointed by the president.

must belong to the same political party as the president.

cannot be elected to more than two consecutive terms.
is the most powerful officer in Congress.

All of the following are delegated powers of Congress EXCEPT:

Group of answer choices

raising and collecting taxes.

declaring war.

granting patents and copyrights.

granting titles of nobility.
granting titles of nobility.

Unlike the House of Representatives, the Senate does not have the power to

Group of answer choices

introduce appropriation bills.

pass laws regulating trade.

try impeached officials.

approve treaties.
introduce appropriation bills.

Which of the following is an implied power of Congress?

Group of answer choices

establishing a national court system

providing for the general welfare of the United States

governing the District of Columbia

borrowing money
providing for the general welfare of the United States

What does it mean that Congress has the power to impeach?

Group of answer choices

Congress can accuse a federal office holder of misconduct.

Congress is permitted to act in a way that betrays or endangers the county if it is protecting all citizens.

Congress can stretch its delegated powers to cover many other areas.

Congress is able to choose the president if no presidential candidate receives enough electoral votes.
Congress can accuse a federal office holder of misconduct.

Congress is not allowed to

Group of answer choices

form the federal court system.

regulate the armed forces.

favor the trade of one state over another state.

make immigration laws.
favor the trade of one state over another state.

Which of the following is NOT a special power of the Senate?

Group of answer choices

All impeachment trials must be held in the Senate.

All bills for raising money must begin in the Senate.

All treaties with foreign nations must be approved by the Senate.

Appointments to the Supreme Court must be approved in the Senate.
All bills for raising money must begin in the Senate.

Who is allowed to generate ideas to start a bill?

Group of answer choices

the president

organized groups

members of Congress

all of the above
all of the above

After a bill is introduced, it is usually

Group of answer choices

sent to the president for approval.

discussed before a Committee of the Whole.

sent to a standing committee.

sent to a joint conference committee.
sent to a standing committee.

Before a bill is approved

Group of answer choices

a committee holds hearings to gather information.

committee members may revise the bill.

the committee makes a recommendation for or against the bill.

all of the above
all of the above

Permanent congressional committees that meet regularly are called

Group of answer choices

select committees.

standing committees.

special sessions.

joint sessions.
standing committees.

Conference committees are formed

Group of answer choices

to gather opinions of the taxpayers regarding a proposed bill.

to debate a bill with the executive branch.

to work out compromises when the House and the Senate pass different versions of the same bill.

to consider several bills addressing the same topic.
to work out compromises when the House and the Senate pass different versions of the same bill.

The Senate and the House of Representatives have passed different versions of an air pollution bill. The kind of committee that would most likely be formed to work out a compromise would be a

Group of answer choices

standing committee.

select committee.

conference committee.

joint committee.
conference committee.

What can happen once Congress decides on a final version of a bill?

Group of answer choices

The president can sign it and declare it a law.

The president may veto it and send it back to Congress with reasons for rejecting it.

The president may veto it and Congress can still pass the bill into law with a two-thirds vote.

all of the above
all of the above

Congress is in session, and the president is given a bill to sign. He lets the bill sit on his desk without action for 10 days. It can be concluded that

Group of answer choices

the bill has become law.

the bill has been vetoed.

the bill will have to go back to committee.

both houses of Congress will have to vote on the bill again.
the bill has become law.
